info.name: VibeMail API
info.description:
Complete request coverage for the VibeMail backend API (CONTRACT.md §4).

## Collection variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `baseUrl` | `http://localhost:3000` | Switch to your Vercel deployment URL for production testing |
| `jwt` | *(empty)* | JWT issued by the OAuth callback. Copy the `token=` param from the redirect URL. |
| `messageId` | `replace_with_real_gmail_message_id` | Gmail message ID from `messages[].gmailId` in a list response. Used by §4.5 requests. |
| `threadId` | `replace_with_real_gmail_thread_id` | Gmail thread ID from `messages[].threadId` in a list response. Used by §4.6 requests. |
| `draftMessageId` | *(empty)* | `message.id` returned by Draft Create (§4.8). Populate before running §4.9 and §4.10 requests. |

Each folder contains one happy-path request and one request per named error code from CONTRACT.md §4.

variable.description: Base URL. Change to your Vercel deployment URL (e.g. https://vibemail-backend.vercel.app) for production testing.
JWT issued by GET /api/v1/auth/google/callback. Complete the OAuth flow and copy the ?token= value from the redirect URL.
A valid Gmail message ID from messages[].gmailId in a GET /api/v1/messages response. Required for PATCH /api/v1/messages/:id requests.
A valid Gmail thread ID from messages[].threadId in a GET /api/v1/messages response. Required for GET /api/v1/threads/:threadId requests.
The message.id returned by POST /api/v1/drafts (= Gmail messageId of the draft). Required for PATCH and DELETE /api/v1/drafts/:id. Populate this after running the Draft Create happy path.

item.name: 4.1 OAuth Callback
item.description:
GET /api/v1/auth/google/callback

Receives the authorization code from Google, exchanges it for OAuth tokens, upserts the user in Supabase, issues a JWT, and redirects the browser to FRONTEND_URL/auth/callback?token=<jwt>.

This endpoint is unauthenticated — no Bearer token required.

query.description: Authorization code returned by Google. Obtain a real code by visiting GET /api/v1/auth/google/initiate (or constructing the Google consent URL manually).
Opaque CSRF token (optional). Must match the value generated during OAuth initiation.

request.description:
Simulates Google redirecting back to the callback URL after the user approves the OAuth consent screen.

Expected response: **302 Found** — Location header set to `FRONTEND_URL/auth/callback?token=<jwt>`

The JWT payload contains: `{ sub, email, name, exp }` (HS256, 7-day expiry). Copy the `token=` param from the Location header and store it in the `jwt` collection variable.

Omits the required `code` query parameter.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "MISSING_CODE", "message": "Required query parameter \"code\" is absent" } }
```

query.description: Sent by Google when the user clicks the Deny button on the consent screen.

request.description:
Google sends `error=access_denied` when the user denies the OAuth consent request.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "OAUTH_DENIED", "message": "User denied the OAuth consent request" } }
```

query.description: An obviously invalid authorization code. Google will reject the token exchange with a 4xx error.

request.description:
Sends a malformed / expired authorization code. The POST to `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token` will return a non-2xx, causing the token exchange to fail.

Expected response: **502 Bad Gateway**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "TOKEN_EXCHANGE_FAILED", "message": "OAuth token exchange failed" } }
```

query.description: Real authorization code. The 503 fires when Google's network is unreachable (ECONNABORTED / ETIMEDOUT / ENOTFOUND), not on a bad code.

request.description:
Triggered when the server cannot reach Google APIs (network timeout — ECONNABORTED, ETIMEDOUT, or ENOTFOUND). Reproduce in a controlled environment by blocking outbound traffic to `*.googleapis.com` on the server.

Expected response: **503 Service Unavailable**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "GMAIL_UNAVAILABLE", "message": "Timed out reaching Google APIs" } }
```

item.name: 4.2 Message List
item.description:
GET /api/v1/messages

Returns a cursor-paginated page of messages stored in Supabase for the authenticated user, newest-first.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`.

item.name: Happy path — first page, default limit

header.description: JWT issued by the OAuth callback.

request.description:
Fetches the first page of messages with default limit (20), no label filter, no cursor.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{
  "messages": [ /* Message[] ordered newest-first */ ],
  "nextCursor": "base64EncodedCursor or null"
}
```
If `nextCursor` is non-null, pass it as the `cursor` query param on the next request.

exec:
: `
No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }
th { text-align: left; padding: 8px; background: #f8f9fa; color: #444; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0; }

item.name: Happy path — second page with cursor

query.description: Base64-encoded cursor returned as `nextCursor` in the previous page response.

request.description:
Fetches the next page by passing the `nextCursor` from the previous response.

Expected response: **200 OK** — same shape as the first page. `nextCursor` will be `null` when the last page is reached.

exec:
: `
No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }
th { text-align: left; padding: 8px; background: #f8f9fa; color: #444; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0; }

item.name: Happy path — filter by INBOX label

query.description: Gmail label ID to filter by. Other common values: SENT, STARRED, UNREAD, SPAM, TRASH.

request.description:
Fetches messages that carry the `INBOX` label. Any Gmail label ID is accepted (INBOX, SENT, STARRED, UNREAD, SPAM, TRASH, or custom label IDs).

Expected response: **200 OK** — same shape, filtered to messages whose `labelIds` array contains `"INBOX"`.

exec:
: `
No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }
th { text-align: left; padding: 8px; background: #f8f9fa; color: #444; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0; }

request.description:
Sends no `Authorization` header.

Expected response: **401 Unauthorized**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "UNAUTHORIZED", "message": "..." } }
```
The same 401 is returned for a malformed token, an expired token, or a signature mismatch.

exec:

No more pages — this is the last page.

header.description: A syntactically invalid JWT — will fail signature verification.

request.description:
Sends a malformed JWT string that cannot be parsed or verified.

Expected response: **401 Unauthorized**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "UNAUTHORIZED", "message": "..." } }
```

exec:

query.description: One above the maximum allowed value (100).

request.description:
Sends `limit=101` which exceeds the maximum of 100.

Expected response: **422 Unprocessable Entity**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "INVALID_LIMIT", "message": "limit must be an integer between 1 and 100" } }
```
Also triggered by `limit=0` (below minimum of 1) or a non-numeric value like `limit=abc`.

query.description: Non-numeric string — NaN after Number() conversion.

request.description:
Sends `limit=abc`, which is not a valid integer.

Expected response: **422 Unprocessable Entity**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "INVALID_LIMIT", "message": "limit must be an integer between 1 and 100" } }
```

request.description:
**Server-side condition** — triggered when the stored Google OAuth token was issued without the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.modify` scope. Reproduce by authenticating with a deliberately scope-limited token.

Expected response: **403 Forbidden**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "SCOPE_MISSING", "message": "..." } }
```

exec:
: `
No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }
th { text-align: left; padding: 8px; background: #f8f9fa; color: #444; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0; }

request.description:
**Server-side condition** — triggered when Gmail API responds with HTTP 429. Reproduce by exhausting Gmail API quota in the Google Cloud Console or by running many requests in rapid succession.

Expected response: **429 Too Many Requests**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "GMAIL_RATE_LIMITED", "message": "..." } }
```

exec:
: `
No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }
th { text-align: left; padding: 8px; background: #f8f9fa; color: #444; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0; }

request.description:
**Server-side condition** — triggered when Gmail API returns any non-2xx status other than 429 (e.g., 500, 503 from Google). Reproduce by revoking the user's OAuth token in the Google Account security settings and then making a request before the server detects the revocation.

Expected response: **502 Bad Gateway**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "GMAIL_LIST_FAILED", "message": "..." } }
```

exec:

No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }

item.name: 4.3 Message Send
item.description:
POST /api/v1/messages

Composes and sends an email via the Gmail API, then persists the sent message in Supabase. Supports new composition and threaded replies.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`.

request.description:
Sends a reply in an existing Gmail thread by including `threadId`. Get a real thread ID from `messages[].threadId` in a list response.

Expected response: **201 Created** — same `{ message: Message }` shape. The returned `message.threadId` will match the `threadId` sent.

Omits the required `to` field. The same error fires if `subject` or `body` is absent, or if any of the three is an empty string.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "MISSING_FIELDS", "message": "Request body must include to, subject, and body" } }
```

Sends `subject` as an empty string. Fields that are present but empty are treated as missing.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "MISSING_FIELDS", "message": "to, subject, and body must not be empty strings" } }
```

Sends `to` as a string that fails RFC 5321 email validation (no `@` domain part).

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "INVALID_RECIPIENT", "message": "to must be a valid email address" } }
```

Sends a valid body but omits the `Authorization` header.

Expected response: **401 Unauthorized**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "UNAUTHORIZED", "message": "..." } }
```

Provides a `threadId` that does not exist in Gmail. Gmail API returns 404, which the server maps to this error.

Expected response: **404 Not Found**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "THREAD_NOT_FOUND", "message": "..." } }
```

**Server-side condition** — triggered when the Gmail `messages.send` API responds with 429. Reproduce by exhausting Gmail send quota.

Expected response: **429 Too Many Requests**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "GMAIL_RATE_LIMITED", "message": "..." } }
```

**Server-side condition** — triggered when Gmail `messages.send` returns any non-2xx status other than 429 (e.g., 500 from Google). Reproduce by revoking the OAuth token or using a token with expired credentials.

Expected response: **502 Bad Gateway**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "GMAIL_SEND_FAILED", "message": "..." } }
```

item.name: 4.5 Message Actions
item.description:
PATCH /api/v1/messages/:id

Modifies Gmail labels and updates the corresponding derived fields in Supabase. Accepts any combination of `read`, `starred`, `archived`, and `trashed` booleans in a single request — at least one field is required.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`. Set the `messageId` collection variable to a real Gmail message ID before running these requests.

request.description:
Removes the UNREAD label and sets is_read = true.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{"message": {"id": "...", "isRead": true, "isStarred": false, "status": "inbox"}}
```

Adds the UNREAD label and sets is_read = false.

Expected response: **200 OK** — same shape with `isRead: false`.

Adds the STARRED label and sets is_starred = true.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{"message": {"id": "...", "isRead": true, "isStarred": true, "status": "inbox"}}
```
Removes the STARRED label and sets is_starred = false.

Removes INBOX and SENT (if present) so the message moves to All Mail only. `status` becomes `'archived'`. On a sent-to-self message both labels are removed so the status correctly reflects archived rather than staying as sent.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{"message": {"id": "...", "isRead": true, "isStarred": false, "status": "archived"}}
```

variable.description: Use the same messageId you just archived.

request.description:
Adds INBOX back to an archived message, restoring it to the inbox. Run this immediately after the archive happy path to round-trip the state.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{"message": {"id": "...", "isRead": true, "isStarred": false, "status": "inbox"}}
```

Adds the TRASH label and removes INBOX. `status` becomes `'trash'`.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{"message": {"id": "...", "isRead": true, "isStarred": false, "status": "trash"}}
```
Removes the TRASH label. `status` becomes `'archived'` (INBOX label is not restored automatically).

item.name: Happy path — multi-action (read + star in one call)

request.description:
Sends multiple action fields in a single request. All label changes are applied in one `messages.modify` call.

Expected response: **200 OK** with `isRead: true, isStarred: true`.

No recognised boolean fields sent — body contains none of: read, starred, archived, trashed.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "INVALID_BODY", "message": "Body must include at least one of: read, starred, archived, trashed" } }
```

item.name: INVALID_BODY — field is string not boolean

request.description:
Sends `read` as a JSON string rather than a boolean.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request** — `INVALID_BODY`.

Omits the Authorization header.

Expected response: **401 Unauthorized** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

ID does not exist in Supabase for this user.

Expected response: **404 Not Found** — `MESSAGE_NOT_FOUND`.

variable.description: Use a message already marked as read. Run the mark-as-read request first, then re-run this one.

request.description:
All provided fields already match the current message state.

Expected response: **409 Conflict** — `ALREADY_IN_STATE`.

**Server-side condition** — Gmail messages.modify returns 429.

Expected response: **429** — `GMAIL_RATE_LIMITED`.

**Server-side condition** — Gmail messages.modify returns a non-2xx other than 429.

Expected response: **502** — `GMAIL_MODIFY_FAILED`.

item.name: 4.8 Draft Create
item.description:
POST /api/v1/drafts

Creates a Gmail draft via the Gmail drafts API and persists it in Supabase with `status = 'draft'`. The response includes `draftId` (Gmail drafts.id) and the full Message shape.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`.

item.name: Happy path — create draft reply in thread

request.description: Creates a draft attached to an existing Gmail thread. `threadId` in the response will match the provided value.

Omits the required `to` field.

Expected response: **400** — `MISSING_FIELDS`.
Expected response: **400** — `INVALID_RECIPIENT`.
Expected response: **401** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

**Server-side condition** — Gmail drafts.create returns non-2xx. Reproduce by revoking the OAuth token.

Expected response: **502** — `GMAIL_DRAFT_FAILED`.

item.name: 4.9 Draft Update
item.description:
PATCH /api/v1/drafts/:id

Updates the content of an existing Gmail draft. `:id` is the Supabase `messages.id` (= Gmail messageId). All body fields are optional — at least one is required.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`. Set `draftMessageId` to the `message.id` returned by Draft Create.

item.name: Happy path — update subject and body

variable.description: message.id from the Draft Create response (= Gmail messageId, not draftId).

item.name: Happy path — update to field only

request.description: Changes only the recipient. Subject and body remain unchanged.

No fields provided.

Expected response: **400** — `INVALID_BODY`.
Expected response: **400** — `INVALID_RECIPIENT`.
Expected response: **404** — `DRAFT_NOT_FOUND`.
Expected response: **401** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

item.name: 4.10 Draft Delete
item.description:
DELETE /api/v1/drafts/:id

Deletes the Gmail draft and removes the Supabase row. Both operations must succeed — if Supabase deletion fails after a successful Gmail deletion, the error is surfaced. Returns 204 No Content on success.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`. Set `draftMessageId` to the `message.id` from the Draft Create response.

variable.description: message.id from the Draft Create response. After this request succeeds, the draft is gone from both Gmail and Supabase.

request.description:
Deletes the draft from Gmail then removes the Supabase row.

Expected response: **204 No Content** — empty body.
Expected response: **404** — `DRAFT_NOT_FOUND`.
Expected response: **401** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

**Server-side condition** — Gmail drafts.delete returns non-2xx. Supabase row is NOT deleted when this fires. Reproduce by revoking the OAuth token.

Expected response: **502** — `GMAIL_DRAFT_FAILED`.

item.name: 4.11 Draft Send
item.description:
POST /api/v1/drafts/:id/send

Sends an existing Gmail draft via `drafts.send` and transitions the Supabase row: `draftId` → null, `gmailId` → new sent message ID, `status` → `'sent'`.

`:id` is the `message.id` from the Draft Create response (= Gmail messageId, not draftId).

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`.

variable.description: message.id from the Draft Create response. After this request the draft is gone and a sent message row exists in its place.

request.description: Expected response: **404** — `DRAFT_NOT_FOUND`.
Expected response: **401** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

**Server-side condition** — Gmail `drafts.send` returns non-2xx. Reproduce by revoking the OAuth token. The Supabase row is NOT modified when this fires.

Expected response: **502** — `GMAIL_DRAFT_FAILED`.

item.name: 4.7 Message Search
item.description:
GET /api/v1/messages/search

Case-insensitive substring search across subject, from, snippet, and to fields of synced messages. Uses the same cursor-based pagination as the message list.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`.

query.description: Search string — matched against subject, from, snippet, and to fields.

request.description:
Returns messages matching 'hello' in any of the searched fields.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{
  "messages": [ /* Message[] ordered newest-first */ ],
  "nextCursor": "base64cursor or null"
}
```

exec:
: `
No more pages — this is the last page.
`;
body { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; margin: 0; padding: 16px; background: #fff; }
th { text-align: left; padding: 8px; background: #f8f9fa; color: #444; font-weight: 600; border-bottom: 2px solid #e0e0e0; }

query.description: nextCursor from a previous search response.

request.description: Fetches the next page of search results using a cursor from the previous response.

request.description:
No `q` query parameter provided.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request**
```json
{ "error": { "code": "MISSING_QUERY", "message": "Query parameter \"q\" is required and must not be empty" } }
```

exec:

request.description:
`q` is an empty string.

Expected response: **400 Bad Request** — `MISSING_QUERY`.

No more pages — this is the last page.

No Authorization header.

Expected response: **401** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

request.description:
`limit=0` is below the minimum of 1.

Expected response: **422** — `INVALID_LIMIT`.

item.name: 4.6 Thread View
item.description:
GET /api/v1/threads/:threadId

Returns all messages in a Gmail thread for the authenticated user, ordered oldest-first. Only messages already synced to Supabase are returned.

Requires `Authorization: Bearer {{jwt}}`. Set the `threadId` collection variable to a real Gmail thread ID (from `messages[].threadId` in a list response).

variable.description: Gmail thread ID — get from messages[].threadId in a list response.

request.description:
Fetches all synced messages in the thread, oldest-first.

Expected response: **200 OK**
```json
{
  "threadId": "...",
  "messages": [ /* Message[] ordered oldest-first */ ]
}
```

No Authorization header.

Expected response: **401** — `UNAUTHORIZED`.

item.name: THREAD_NOT_FOUND — no messages for this thread

request.description:
Thread ID that exists in Gmail but has no messages synced for this user.

Expected response: **404** — `THREAD_NOT_FOUND`.
